scenario: Serious AE (hospitalization criterion) — HCP-reported drug-induced liver injury on CholoClear-X. Complete ICSR (all 4 pillars). Routes to PV via Gate 4; 15-day serious clock. Different serious criterion from case_008 (life-threatening) and case_021 (cardiac/IME).
input_text:
SOURCE CHANNEL: Email Inbound Gateway
PRIMARY DISPATCH LOG: safety-intake@cardiomedglobal.com

MESSAGE BODY:
To CardioMed Drug Safety,

I am reporting a serious adverse event in one of my patients, a 64-year-old male, who has been taking your product CholoClear-X for hypercholesterolemia. Approximately three weeks after starting therapy he developed progressive jaundice, dark urine, and severe right-upper-quadrant pain. Labs showed markedly elevated transaminases (ALT 880, AST 740) and bilirubin. He was admitted to our hospital for acute drug-induced liver injury and remains inpatient under monitoring. His only concomitant medication is lisinopril for hypertension.

Please confirm receipt for your safety database.

Nathan Cole, MD
Riverside Hepatology Associates
Email: ncole@riversidehep.org | Phone: 415-555-0188

failed_gates: Gate 4: Adverse-event policy — AE flag set -> PV (never auto-approve)

labeling_notes: SERIOUS by the HOSPITALIZATION criterion (ICH E2A) — the patient was admitted as an inpatient for acute liver injury. Distinct from case_008 (life-threatening anaphylaxis) and case_021 (cardiac/IME), giving the golden set coverage across multiple serious criteria.

COMPLETE ICSR: identifiable patient (64M), identifiable reporter (Dr. Cole, in HCP registry), suspect product (CholoClear-X), and event (DILI) -> all four pillars True -> is_valid_icsr True -> submission-ready.

onset_date null: onset is given relatively ('three weeks after starting'), not as a calendar date -> per the extraction rule, onset_date is null. patient_initials null: the reporter gives age/sex but no initials.
ROUTING: Gate 4 sends every AE to PV. Gate 6 passes (Dr. Cole resolves to NET_HCP_62890). Native-digital so Gate 1 passes via the no-OCR path.
